what_it_means: Py-Feat pipeline: face detection → landmarks → action units → emotion classification
Total number of videos processed in this run
% of videos where predicted valence matched ground truth. Baseline (always neutral) = 33.3%.
Average F1 across all 3 classes equally. Balances precision and recall.
Agreement beyond chance. 0=random, 1=perfect. Accounts for prediction imbalance.
Average precision across classes. Of all videos predicted as class X, how many actually were X?
Average recall across classes. Of all actual class X videos, how many did the model catch?
F1 for POSITIVE class. Low = model struggles to detect happy faces.
F1 for NEUTRAL class. Low = model confuses resting faces with emotions.
F1 for NEGATIVE class. Low = model misses or over-predicts upset faces.
AUC for positive vs rest using score_positive. 0.5=random, 1.0=perfect.
AUC for neutral vs rest using score_neutral.
AUC for negative vs rest using score_negative.
Average AUC across all 3 classes. Above 0.7=acceptable, above 0.8=good.
Average processing time per video in ms. Relevant for real-time robot deployment.
Median latency — half of videos processed faster than this.
95th percentile latency — worst-case speed for 95% of videos.
